bead.title: lifecycle: split openChildCount/totalChildCount in isOrdinaryEpicContainer
bead.description:
PROBLEM
ddx work plan reports "skipped 10 ready epic(s) with open children" for epics that in fact have zero open children. 6 of 10 epics in the current project (ddx-6862f2ac, ddx-83440482, ddx-ca1c66ee, ddx-cc0641a7, ddx-ccda7a32, ddx-eeced112) have totalChildCount==0 or all children closed, but they are bucketed as LifecycleBucketEpicContainer rather than LifecycleBucketEpicClosureCandidate. This blocks the FEAT-010 auto-remediation path because the diagnoser cannot distinguish closure-pending from genuinely-undecomposed.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/lifecycle.go:218 isOrdinaryEpicContainer (and its call site at cli/internal/bead/store.go:1853) checks childCount[b.ID] without distinguishing open from closed children. Any epic with at least one historical child is bucketed as a container, even if every child is closed.

PROPOSED FIX
In cli/internal/bead/lifecycle.go, change isOrdinaryEpicContainer (and the surrounding classifier) to take both openChildCount and totalChildCount as separate signals. Routing rule:
- IssueType==epic AND totalChildCount==0 → leaves the container bucket; bead is eligible for genuinely_needs_decomposition diagnosis at the work-loop layer (do not change the classifier to a new bucket here — just stop putting it in LifecycleBucketEpicContainer).
- IssueType==epic AND openChildCount==0 AND totalChildCount>0 → LifecycleBucketEpicClosureCandidate (the existing closure path).
- IssueType==epic AND openChildCount>0 → LifecycleBucketEpicContainer (unchanged).

In cli/internal/bead/store.go around the call site (~line 1853), update the call to pass both counts. Grep for every reader of LifecycleBucketEpicContainer (cli/internal/bead/*.go, cli/cmd/work*.go, cli/internal/agent/*.go) and verify no caller relies on the old "any-historical-children" semantics; if any does, give it the correct field rather than the bucket.

NON-SCOPE
- Adding a new lifecycle bucket. Closure candidates already have LifecycleBucketEpicClosureCandidate.
- Wiring auto-remediation. That happens in the work-loop bead.
- Behavior changes for non-epic beads.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-2a1ece2b. No deps.
bead.acceptance:
1. cli/internal/bead/lifecycle.go isOrdinaryEpicContainer (or the surrounding classifier function) takes openChildCount and totalChildCount as separate signals; closure-candidate routing requires openChildCount==0 && totalChildCount>0; totalChildCount==0 epics do not enter LifecycleBucketEpicContainer.
2. New test TestIsOrdinaryEpicContainer_ZeroOpenChildrenRoutesToClosureCandidate in cli/internal/bead/lifecycle_test.go covers: (a) epic with openChildCount==0, totalChildCount>0 → closure candidate; (b) epic with totalChildCount==0 → neither bucket; (c) epic with openChildCount>0 → container; (d) non-epic bead with children → unaffected.
3. Every caller of LifecycleBucketEpicContainer in the cli/ tree is audited (grep documented in the PR body); no caller relies on the old historical-count semantics. If any did, it is updated to read the explicit field instead.
4. After this lands, running ddx work plan against the current repo state shows ddx-6862f2ac, ddx-83440482, ddx-ca1c66ee, ddx-cc0641a7, ddx-ccda7a32, ddx-eeced112 surfacing under closure-candidate output, not under "ready epic(s) with open children".
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... -count=1 green.
6. cd cli && go test ./... green (no regressions).
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
